prompt:
Rewrite the following paragraph from a technical engineering blog post to be more engaging. The original is bland and generic. Save the rewrite to rewrite.md in the current working directory. Don't ask follow-up questions; commit to a rewrite.

Original:
'Performance was significantly improved across the board after the migration. Many of our queries now run much faster, which has had a positive impact on user experience. The team is happy with the results and we plan to continue making improvements in this area.'
assertions: rewrite.md contains at least TWO specific numeric measurements (e.g. '120ms', '3x', '40%', 'P99', or similar) — replacing the abstract adjectives in the original ('significantly', 'much', 'many'). Synonyms like 'considerably faster' or 'dramatically improved' do NOT count — the assertion requires actual digits.

rewrite.md names at least ONE specific concrete entity that didn't appear in the original — examples: a database (Postgres, MySQL), an endpoint (/orders, /search, the checkout API), a system component, a tool, or a measurement context. Generic terms ('queries', 'the system', 'our app', 'users') do NOT count — the assertion requires a proper noun, an endpoint path, or a versioned tool name.

The rewrite avoids the AI-prose tell: the literal words 'significantly', 'much faster', 'positive impact', and 'happy with the results' do NOT appear anywhere in rewrite.md (case-insensitive). At least one of those four phrases would survive a baseline rewrite that just polishes; this assertion fails when the rewrite is cosmetic instead of substantive.
